Use the olko:xquik-x-automation skill.


# xquik-x-automation

Use this skill when a user asks an agent to work with X/Twitter data through Xquik: search tweets, inspect users, export result sets, set up monitors or webhooks, prepare posting workflows, or connect an agent to the Xquik MCP server.

Do not use this skill for harassment, spam, credential collection, evasion, or broad surveillance. Keep every workflow bounded by the user's stated task and the target account or query they approved.

## Workflow

1. Define the task boundary:
  - read-only research
  - export or bulk extraction
  - persistent monitor or webhook
  - draft, post, follow, unfollow, or profile update workflow
2. Choose the right Xquik surface:
  - Agent skill: install the public `x-twitter-scraper` skill when the agent needs procedural guidance and endpoint references.
  - REST API: use the public API docs for scripts, backfills, dashboards, and product integrations.
  - MCP: use the MCP docs when the agent already works through an MCP-capable client.
3. Install the public skill when local agent guidance is needed:

```bash
npx skills@1.5.3 add Xquik-dev/x-twitter-scraper@v2.4.16
```

4. Load only the relevant public reference:
  - API overview: `https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/overview`
  - MCP overview: `https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/overview`
  - Skill source: `https://github.com/Xquik-dev/x-twitter-scraper/tree/master/skills/x-twitter-scraper`
5. For reads and exports, estimate scope before running:
  - query or account
  - date range
  - maximum result count
  - fields needed in the output
  - export format
6. For monitors and webhooks, confirm persistent-resource details first:
  - monitored account or keyword query
  - event types
  - delivery destination
  - stop condition or review date
7. For writes, require explicit user confirmation for the exact action payload before calling any write endpoint.
8. Report results with source context:
  - endpoint or MCP tool used
  - query or account inspected
  - time range and limit
  - output file or destination
  - follow-up actions that still need user approval

## Verification

- Confirm the Xquik skill install command or public skill source path resolves.
- Confirm docs links return 200 before linking them in user-facing output.
- For generated files, record the file path and row count or object count.
- For monitors and webhooks, record the monitor or webhook identifier and the confirmed target.
- For writes, record only the public result identifier or URL. Do not print API keys, cookies, or raw session material.

## Public References

- Xquik skill source: `https://github.com/Xquik-dev/x-twitter-scraper/tree/master/skills/x-twitter-scraper`
- Xquik API docs: `https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/overview`
- Xquik MCP docs: `https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/overview`
